title: Adani Group trio improve ESG ratings for 3rd consecutive year - BusinessLine
content:
Three Adani Group energy companies—Adani Power, Adani Energy Solutions and Adani Green Energy—have simultaneously achieved significant improvements in their Morningstar Sustainalytics ESG Risk Ratings. This is third consecutive year of improvement in ESG scores for the companies, stated an official release here, Monday.
Morningstar Sustainalytics, the US-headquartered independent ESG ratings firm, has reaffirmed the Group’s stability and transparency by lowering risk scores across the board, the company added. Adani Power has moved from high to medium risk, Adani Energy Solutions has been upgraded from medium to low risk, while Adani Green Energy, which was already in the low category, has been rated even lower.
A low ESG risk score signals that a company is less exposed to potential disruptions arising from regulatory changes, social unrest or environmental challenges. For investors, this translates into greater stability, responsible management and the promise of sustainable long-term returns, while also enhancing market trust and access to global capital. The ratings place Adani Green and Adani Energy Solutions as seventh and eighth ranking, respectively worldwide among electrical utilities
The achievement also places the three Adani companies well ahead of their Indian peers. Adani Power’s risk score of 29.2 outperforms Tata Power, NTPC, JSW Energy, NLC India and Torrent Power, all of which remain in the higher-risk band. Adani Green Energy, with a score of 11.8, stands ahead of ReNew at 13.1 and far ahead of Waaree Renewable at 27.7. Adani Energy Solutions at 19.9 leads over PGCIL and CESC, while the sectoral average remains at a much higher 36.
These rankings make the Adani trio the highest-rated Indian companies in their categories and among the global elite in governance and sustainability. Morningstar Sustainalytics conducts its assessments independently, drawing solely on publicly available disclosures made by companies. The consistent improvement in the ratings of the three Adani energy businesses reinforces the recent SEBI findings that dismissed a short-seller’s allegations, which had already been disproved at multiple regulatory and judicial forums, the release added.
Adani Energy Solutions and Adani Green Energy are already certified Zero-Waste-to-Landfill, Single-Use-Plastic-Free and Net Water Positive. Adani Power has set new benchmarks in water efficiency with the lowest consumption among comparable plants. Adani Green Energy, India’s largest renewable energy company, now operates more than 16.5 GW of capacity, underscoring the Group’s alignment with the global energy transition.
Published on October 6, 2025
